title: Earthï؟½s Immortalities
content:
آ آ آ آ Fame

آ آ آ آ See, as the prettiest graves will do in time,
آ آ آ آ Our poetï؟½s wants the freshness of its prime;
آ آ آ آ Spite of the sextonï؟½s browsing horse, the sods
آ آ آ آ Have struggled throï؟½ its binding osier-rods;
آ آ آ آ Headstone and half-sunk footstone lean awry,
آ آ آ آ Wanting the brick-work promised by-and-by;
آ آ آ آ How the minute grey lichens, plate oï؟½er plate,
آ آ آ آ Have softened down the crisp-cut name and date!



آ آ آ آ Love

آ آ آ آ So, the yearï؟½s done with
آ آ آ آ (Love me for ever!)
آ آ آ آ All March begun with,
آ آ آ آ Aprilï؟½s endeavour;
آ آ آ آ May-wreaths that bound me
آ آ آ آ June needs must sever;
آ آ آ آ Now snows fall round me,
آ آ آ آ Quenching Juneï؟½s fever,
آ آ آ آ (Love me for ever!)
meaning:
"Earthï؟½s Immortalities" is a quintessential example of Robert Browning's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
